reason: Core logic meets FR-05 requirements: phone (mobile + landline), email, and Taiwan address patterns are detected and masked; PIIMaskResult(masked_text, mask_count, pii_types) is returned correctly; should_escalate() covers all four AC-specified keywords (密碼, 銀行帳戶, 信用卡號, 提款卡) via _SENSITIVE_KEYWORDS superset; credit card back-to-front replacement (PIIMaskingV2) explicitly uses reversed(matches) per AC. One spec-drift gap: AC specifies token strings [phone_masked], [email_masked], [address_masked] but implementation emits [PHONE], [EMAIL], [ADDR] — tests are written to match the implementation rather than the spec, so tests pass but do not enforce the AC-specified token format. This is a cosmetic/spec-conformance deviation, not a functional regression.
gaps: Mask token format mismatch: AC specifies [phone_masked]/[email_masked]/[address_masked]; implementation uses [PHONE]/[EMAIL]/[ADDR]. Tests verify [PHONE] not [phone_masked], so they pass but do not enforce the SRS-specified token strings (pii/__init__.py:112-120, tests/test_fr05.py:27-35).
